cards.h3Text: CTRFHub: building a CTRF-native test reporting platform in the open
cards.excerptText: Why we're building a test reporting tool that assumes CTRF as its primary input, what's in the box today, and how to follow along on GitHub.

BADCamp 2020 — Components can break your site, part 2.

We all benefit from open source.

cards.h3Text: Version 1.0 of Automated Testing Kit is ready.
cards.excerptText: After many months of development, ATK has settled down enough to release version 1.0. Here's the philosophy behind it and what made it into the first stable release.

cards.h3Text: Introducing Automated Testing Kit.
cards.excerptText: A first look at ATK — a shared set of Playwright and Cypress tests for Drupal sites that exercises the basic operations every site needs to keep working.

cards.h3Text: Cypress on Drupal cheat sheet.
cards.excerptText: A printable reference for the commands and selectors we reach for most often when writing Cypress tests against Drupal sites.

cards.h3Text: BADCamp 2020 — Components can break your site, part 2.
cards.excerptText: A follow-up to the DrupalCon talk: more failure modes for Layout Builder components, with concrete examples of what to test for before they reach production.

cards.h3Text: Our talk at DrupalCon: Layout Builder components can break your site. Here's how.
cards.excerptText: There is a danger with Layout Builder that can bite you if you aren't careful. We walked through it on the DrupalCon stage and recap the failure patterns here.

cards.h3Text: We all benefit from open source.
cards.excerptText: A short note on why we contribute the testing tools we depend on back to the Drupal community — and why that's not charity, it's how the work gets done.
